title: Lore Unified Settings
description: User-config schema for ~/.lore/config/settings.json. Authoritative shape for valid keys, types, and value constraints across the bash/Python/Go loaders. Closed-set rejection at the boundary: unknown frameworks, role ids, capability ids, and tui layouts must be rejected — never silently routed to a default. Migration provenance lives in ~/.lore/.install-state/migration.json (D4) and MUST NOT appear inside settings.json — additionalProperties:false on every object enforces this implicitly. Cross-field invariants (e.g. active_framework value must be present in harnesses) live in the loaders and `lore doctor`, not here. The active_framework enum and harnesses keyset MUST stay in lockstep with adapters/capabilities.json frameworks (covered by tests/frameworks/settings_schema_keyset_parity.bats); $defs/role_id MUST stay in lockstep with adapters/roles.json (same test).

version.description: Schema/file version. Increment when a breaking shape change ships. install.sh consults this against its cleanup-version constant before deleting the legacy fragmented files (D4 phase 3).

active_framework.description: Active harness id. The named framework must also have a populated `harnesses.<name>` block — this cross-field invariant is enforced by the loaders and `lore doctor`, not by this schema.

harnesses.description: Per-harness configuration. Keyset is closed against adapters/capabilities.json frameworks — adding a new harness requires schema bump + capabilities.json entry + install.sh allowlist update (no defaults, no fall-through).

capability_overrides.description: Operator overrides for capability support levels (D8). Keyset is closed against adapters/capabilities.json `capabilities` map — values must be one of full|partial|fallback|none. The runtime semantics (override applies to the active framework) are preserved from the legacy framework.json shape; the documented defect that overrides silently re-apply across active_framework changes is intentionally NOT fixed in this consolidation — see plan D3.

roles.description: Legacy top-level role -> model fallback. New settings should store role bindings under `harnesses.<name>.roles` because model defaults are harness-specific; this field remains valid for migration/deprecation only and is hidden from the TUI.

ceremonies.description: Legacy top-level skill -> [advisor, ...] fallback. New settings should store ceremony advisor bindings under `harnesses.<name>.ceremonies` because ceremony skill defaults are harness-specific; this field remains valid for migration/deprecation only and is hidden from the TUI.

capture.description: Probabilistic capture / structural-signal thresholds — historical schema from stop-novelty-check.py (retired 2026-05-06). Kept for backward compat with operators who still have ~/.lore/config/capture-config.json on disk; no live consumer reads these fields after retirement.

settlement.description: Probabilistic post-ceremony audit-trigger configuration. Sourced from the legacy ~/.lore/config/settlement-config.json shape — the schema_version field is dropped here in favor of the unified top-level `version`.

probabilistic_triggers.description: Per-skill audit-fire probability. Keyset is open: any skill id from adapters/capabilities.json `skills` map is valid. Keys not listed default to 0 (never fire). Property keys are kebab-case skill ids.

background_queue.description: When enabled and ~/.lore/scripts/background-queue-enqueue.sh exists, fired triggers route through $KDIR/_work-queue/audit/ for asynchronous dispatch (task-28). Otherwise falls back to detached-child Popen (task-29). The `description` string is permitted because settlement-config.json shipped with one.

tui.description: TUI preferences. Sourced from the legacy ~/.lore/config/tui.json shape.

layout.description: Pane layout. Closed-set against tui/internal/config/config.go LayoutMode (LayoutLeftRight/LayoutTopBottom).

obsidian.description: Obsidian vault mirror configuration. Multi-vault keyed shape: each entry under `vaults` binds one knowledge base to one vault. The map key is the relative path under `$LORE_DATA_DIR/repos/` (i.e. `resolve-repo.sh` output minus the prefix), so the same identifier the install-base enumeration uses is what addresses a vault binding here. Per D-multi-vault: `enabled: false` retains `vault_path` so re-enable is a single toggle (mirrors `agent.enabled`); a missing `obsidian.vaults.<kb-key>` entry is the canonical 'sync disabled for this KB' state. The legacy single-vault shape (`obsidian.{vault_path,repo_path}`) was retired in favor of this map; readers fall back to the legacy `~/.lore/config/obsidian.json` only when its `repo_path` matches the current KDIR.

enabled.description: Per-KB sync toggle. Absence is treated as `true` for forward-compat — adding `enabled` to an entry is opt-in disable, not opt-in enable.

vault_path.description: Absolute filesystem path to the Obsidian vault directory. Empty string is REJECTED — its semantics are ambiguous (suppress vs. inherit) and doctor flags it as malformed.

vaults.description: Map of knowledge-base key → vault binding. Keyset is open at the schema layer (key shape matches the KB-relative-path produced by `resolve-repo.sh`); doctor cross-references against the install-base.

framework_id.description: Closed framework registry. MUST stay in lockstep with adapters/capabilities.json `frameworks` keyset — drift is caught by tests/frameworks/settings_schema_keyset_parity.bats.

role_id.description: Closed role registry. MUST stay in lockstep with adapters/roles.json `roles[].id` — drift is caught by tests/frameworks/settings_schema_keyset_parity.bats. Adding a role requires updating roles.json AND this enum AND scripts/lib.sh resolve_model_for_role AND tui/internal/config/config.go ResolveModelForRole AND tests/frameworks/roles.bats.

role_value.description: Model id bound to a role (e.g. "opus", "sonnet", "haiku", or a fully-qualified provider/model like "anthropic/sonnet"). Empty string is REJECTED — its semantics are ambiguous (suppress vs. use-default) and it would route through resolve_model_for_role as a non-empty truthy value in jq. The schema forbids it; doctor surfaces violations.

skill_id.description: Kebab-case skill id (e.g. "spec-design", "pr-review", "codex-plan-review"). Open at the schema layer — closed-set rejection against adapters/capabilities.json `skills` map happens in the loaders and doctor, not here. Pattern blocks obvious typos and uppercase/whitespace mistakes.

advisor_id.description: Kebab-case advisor id (e.g. "codex-design-review", "codex-plan-review", "codex-pr-review"). Open at the schema layer — closed-set rejection against the skills registry happens in the loaders and doctor. Today the open shape matches scripts/ceremony-config.sh which performs no closed-set validation.

roles_map_top.description: Top-level role bindings. Closed-keyset against $defs/role_id. `default` is required so resolve_model_for_role's terminal fallback never returns undefined. Other roles are optional (absence falls through to `default`).

roles_map_overlay.description: Per-harness role -> model bindings. Closed-keyset against $defs/role_id. All keys optional except where callers need a concrete default; absence falls through to legacy top-level `roles` during the deprecation window.

ceremonies_map.description: Skill -> advisor list. Property keys are kebab-case skill ids; values are arrays of kebab-case advisor ids. Empty array is valid and means "run with no advisors". Absent key means no ceremony configured at that layer; runtime may still consult legacy fallback data during the deprecation window. Keyset is open at the schema layer; closed-set rejection against the skills registry lives in the loaders and doctor.

additionalProperties.description: Empty array = explicit suppression override (stops fall-through). Non-empty = explicit advisor list. Absent key = fall-through to the next layer.

harness_block.description: Per-harness configuration block. `args` is required (every harness has a CLI arg list, possibly empty). `enabled`, `roles`, and `ceremonies` are optional for migration compatibility. New settings should include `roles` and `ceremonies` here because agent defaults and ceremony skills vary by harness. Absent `enabled` is default-on (matches the legacy global `agent.enabled` semantic); explicit `false` disables this harness's integration.

args.description: CLI args prepended to every harness invocation. Empty array is valid (no flags). For claude-code the legacy default was ["--dangerously-skip-permissions"]; other harnesses default to [].

enabled.description: Per-harness integration toggle. When false, scripts/harness-toggle/disable.sh has removed this harness's skill/agent symlinks and cleared its instruction-file lore region. Absent = enabled (default-on, matches the legacy `agent.enabled` semantic). Mutated only via `lore harness {enable,disable}`; direct settings.json patches that bypass the toggle scripts are rejected by the unified loaders to keep symlink/instruction-file state in sync with the flag.

capability_id.description: Closed capability registry. MUST stay in lockstep with adapters/capabilities.json `capabilities` map — drift is caught by tests/frameworks/settings_schema_keyset_parity.bats.

capability_overrides_map.description: Capability -> support-level overrides. Closed-keyset against $defs/capability_id. Empty object is the default (no overrides).
